blurb: Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of its "A Garden with Wings" exhibit this Saturday with Pollinator Palooza, a full day of gardens, butterflies, and native plant education from 10am to 5pm. It's a genuinely beautiful property and a good slow-morning kind of outing. This one skews toward nature lovers, gardeners, and anyone who wants a reason to be outside without a crowd pressing in on all sides. The anniversary makes this year's edition worth noting. Check the Smith-Gilbert Gardens site for admission details.
scoring_notes: The 10-year anniversary of the signature exhibit gives this recurring event a genuine uniqueness bump. Skews toward the gardening and nature-curious segment of East Cobb's audience, which is real but not the majority. Good for the right reader, not a universal crowd-pleaser.
